summary: Closed M2-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 after PR #51 merged into main. The M2 milestone exit gate is integrated at main merge commit eb8039b5a038c9ed5a29a88e09bdc33699c4c2cd; project state now advances to M3 under the existing autonomous-goal policy, with M5 still reserved as a Human Gate. M3 implementation work is not started by this closure and still requires an accepted M3 task graph.

tests_run.result: PR #51 MERGED at 2026-06-25T03:53:49Z with merge commit eb8039b5a038c9ed5a29a88e09bdc33699c4c2cd; Quality, Web, and Browser Smoke succeeded in 1m28s and Windows Desktop Smoke Artifact succeeded in 48s.
M2-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 merge commit is an ancestor of origin/main.
Before this closure edit, M2-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 was ACCEPTED and all prior M2 dependency tasks were CLOSED.
No dependency-ready task printed after the M2 gate PR entered main; M3 task graph creation is the next orchestration step.

decisions: Close M2-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 only after confirming the accepted gate entered origin/main through PR #51.
Advance current_milestone from M2 to M3 in the post-merge closure commit.
Do not start M3 implementation in this closure commit.
Require a future accepted M3 task graph before M3 implementation work begins.
Keep M5 as a formal Human Gate under MILESTONE-AUTONOMY-AMENDMENT-001.
risks: No M3 task graph exists in the repository yet; autonomous continuation should create or validate that graph before spawning M3 implementation writers.
M2 H-001/H-002 evidence remains accepted only as initial milestone evidence, not final full-scale release proof.
requested_action: After this closure commit is integrated, create or validate the M3 task graph before starting M3 implementation work.
